article_title: Biome v2 Ships Type-Aware JavaScript Linting Without the TypeScript Compiler
summary: Submission approved with corrections: minor mischaracterization of Reddit community comments in body

findings.details: The article describes Reddit commenters as criticizing the 'migration path as poor' when the cited InfoQ source (source-3) shows those comments are about switching to Biome/Oxlint due to ESLint being a 'performance hog'. The InfoQ article itself calls migration 'straightforward'.
The article attributes '~95% rule coverage' as InfoQ's characterization, but this figure comes from a Reddit commenter's opinion quoted by InfoQ, not from InfoQ's own editorial assessment.

content_preview.title: Biome v2 Ships Type-Aware JavaScript Linting Without the TypeScript Compiler
content_preview.summary: Biome v2, released June 2025, introduces type-aware lint rules and a plugin system without requiring tsc, reaching v2.4 by February 2026.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Biome, the Rust-based web toolchain that has positioned itself as a unified replacement for ESLint and Prettier, shipped its v2.0.0 release on June 17, 2025, according to the [project's GitHub releases page](https://github.com/biomejs/biome/releases). The major release introduced what the project describes as the first type-aware linting for JavaScript and TypeScript that does not rely on the TypeScript compiler, alongside a new plugin system built on the GritQL query language. By F...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured article covering Biome v2's major features including type-aware linting, GritQL plugin system, and domains. Appropriate depth for a News category piece at 896 words.
editor_notes.source_verification: source-0.html.gz (github.com/biomejs/biome): Confirmed 24.7k stars, 86.3% Rust codebase, MIT/Apache 2.0 licensing. source-1.html.gz (github.com/biomejs/biome/releases): Confirmed v2.4.15 on May 9, 2026. v2.0.0 and v2.4.0 dates not explicitly found in the snapshot but release history structure is consistent. source-2.html.gz (infoq.com/news/2025/06/Biome-Releases-v2-Beta): Confirmed GritQL plugin system, 'plenty of ideas for making them more powerful' quote, 35x faster than Prettier claim, domains concept, Rome lineage. noFloatingPromises as the first type-aware rule is NOT found in this source — it is a specific claim that cannot be verified from available snapshots. source-3.html.gz (infoq.com/news/2026/04/eslint-10-release): Confirmed 120 million weekly npm downloads, Biome/Oxlint as Rust-based alternatives, Oxlint 50-100x faster claim. The 95% figure comes from a Reddit commenter quoted by InfoQ, not InfoQ's own characterization. The article misframes community Reddit comments about ESLint performance as migration friction criticism.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core claims about Biome v2 features are consistent with sources. Two minor misattributions in a subordinate section: (1) '~95% rule coverage' presented as InfoQ's characterization when it is a Reddit commenter's opinion; (2) Reddit comments characterized as criticizing the 'migration path as poor' when the actual quoted comment was about switching to Biome due to ESLint performance. The InfoQ ESLint article describes migration as 'straightforward'. The noFloatingPromises claim is not verifiable from snapshots but is plausible technical context.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism or editorializing. Appropriate use of hedging language in 'What We Don't Know' section.
editor_notes.originality: No existing coverage of Biome v2 in the archive. Original topic.
concerns: The article mischaracterizes Reddit community feedback on ESLint v10 — framing comments about switching to alternatives as criticism of the migration path specifically, when the InfoQ source does not support this framing. The InfoQ article calls migration 'straightforward'.
'~95% rule coverage' is attributed to InfoQ's characterization but is actually a Reddit commenter's opinion quoted by InfoQ.
recommendations: Corrections record should note the misattribution of the migration friction narrative and the 95% figure attribution.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively solid article on a genuine developer tools story. The core claims about Biome v2 features, versioning timeline, and competitive landscape are well-sourced. Two minor misattributions in a subordinate competitive landscape paragraph warrant a corrections record.
